content:
# Spatial Entropy

**Summary**: A measure of the spatial distribution of information or complexity within an image.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2015-mohammed|Mohammed, 2015]] used spatial entropy as a component of a non-reference quality assessment method.

## Scientific Context

- [[2015-mohammed|Mohammed, 2015]] Used to objectively quantify image quality without a reference sample.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2015-mohammed|Mohammed, 2015]] Calculated as part of the SSEQ framework.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Spatial entropy serves as a metric to evaluate the amount of blur and noise in seismic images. ([[2015-mohammed|Mohammed, 2015, p. 14]])
